1: {
2:   "project": {
3:     "name": "langchainrb",
4:     "description": "Ruby gem for building LLM-backed applications. It exposes a unified Assistant that can hold tool-calling conversations with multiple external LLM providers.",
5:     "autoloading": "Rails Engine with Zeitwerk (lib/langchain/engine.rb). Files under lib/langchain/ are autoloaded by the host Rails app; no explicit require list in lib/langchain.rb."
6:   },
7:   "step1_orient": {
8:     "summary": "langchainrb is a Rails engine that wraps external LLM APIs. The Assistant (lib/langchain/assistant.rb) orchestrates tool-calling conversations. For each provider there is an LLM client (lib/langchain/llm/*.rb), a response wrapper (lib/langchain/llm/response/*.rb), an Assistant adapter (lib/langchain/assistant/llm/adapters/*.rb), and a message formatter (lib/langchain/assistant/messages/*.rb). Shared machinery lives in Langchain::LLM::Base, Langchain::LLM::Response::BaseResponse, Langchain::Assistant::LLM::Adapters::Base, Langchain::Assistant::Messages::Base, Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat, and Langchain::ToolDefinition.",

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   # Assistants are Agent-like objects that leverage helpful instructions, LLMs, tools and knowledge to respond to user queries.
5:   # Assistants can be configured with an LLM of your choice, any vector search database and easily extended with additional tools.
6:   #
7:   # Usage:
8:   #     llm = Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini.new(api_key: ENV["GOOGLE_GEMINI_API_KEY"])
9:   #     assistant = Langchain::Assistant.new(
10:   #       llm: llm,
11:   #       instructions: "You're a News Reporter AI",
12:   #       tools: [Langchain::Tool::NewsRetriever.new(api_key: ENV["NEWS_API_KEY"])]
13:   #     )
14:   class Assistant
15:     attr_reader :llm,
16:       :instructions,
17:       :state,
18:       :llm_adapter,
19:       :messages,
20:       :tool_choice,
21:       :total_prompt_tokens,
22:       :total_completion_tokens,
23:       :total_tokens
24: 
25:     attr_accessor :tools,
26:       :add_message_callback,
27:       :tool_execution_callback,
28:       :parallel_tool_calls
29: 
30:     # Create a new assistant
31:     #
32:     # @param llm [Langchain::LLM::Base] LLM instance that the assistant will use
33:     # @param tools [Array<Langchain::Tool::Base>] Tools that the assistant has access to
34:     # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
35:     # @param tool_choice [String] Specify how tools should be selected. Options: "auto", "any", "none", or <specific function name>
36:     # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether or not to run tools in parallel
37:     # @param messages [Array<Langchain::Assistant::Messages::Base>] The messages
38:     # @param add_message_callback [Proc] A callback function (Proc or lambda) that is called when any message is added to the conversation
39:     # @param tool_execution_callback [Proc] A callback function (Proc or lambda) that is called right before a tool function is executed
40:     def initialize(
41:       llm:,
42:       tools: [],
43:       instructions: nil,
44:       tool_choice: "auto",
45:       parallel_tool_calls: true,
46:       messages: [],
47:       # Callbacks
48:       add_message_callback: nil,
49:       tool_execution_callback: nil,
50:       &block
51:     )
52:       unless tools.is_a?(Array) && tools.all? { |tool| tool.class.singleton_class.include?(Langchain::ToolDefinition) }
53:         raise ArgumentError, "Tools must be an array of objects extending Langchain::ToolDefinition"
54:       end
55: 
56:       @llm = llm
57:       @llm_adapter = LLM::Adapter.build(llm)
58: 
59:       @add_message_callback = add_message_callback if validate_callback!("add_message_callback", add_message_callback)
60:       @tool_execution_callback = tool_execution_callback if validate_callback!("tool_execution_callback", tool_execution_callback)
61: 
62:       self.messages = messages
63:       @tools = tools
64:       @parallel_tool_calls = parallel_tool_calls
65:       self.tool_choice = tool_choice
66:       self.instructions = instructions
67:       @block = block
68:       @state = :ready
69: 
70:       @total_prompt_tokens = 0
71:       @total_completion_tokens = 0
72:       @total_tokens = 0
73:     end
74: 
75:     # Add a user message to the messages array
76:     #
77:     # @param role [String] The role attribute of the message. Default: "user"
78:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
79:     # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image to include in the message
80:     # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to include in the message
81:     # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to include in the message
82:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
83:     def add_message(role: "user", content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
84:       message = build_message(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
85: 
86:       # Call the callback with the message
87:       add_message_callback.call(message) if add_message_callback # rubocop:disable Style/SafeNavigation
88: 
89:       # Prepend the message to the messages array
90:       messages << message
91: 
92:       @state = :ready
93: 
94:       messages
95:     end
96: 
97:     # Convert messages to an LLM APIs-compatible array of hashes
98:     #
99:     # @return [Array<Hash>] Messages as an OpenAI API-compatible array of hashes
100:     def array_of_message_hashes
101:       messages
102:         .map(&:to_hash)
103:         .compact
104:     end
105: 
106:     # Only used by the Assistant when it calls the LLM#complete() method
107:     def prompt_of_concatenated_messages
108:       messages.map(&:to_s).join
109:     end
110: 
111:     # Set multiple messages
112:     #
113:     # @param messages [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages to set
114:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
115:     def messages=(messages)
116:       raise ArgumentError, "messages array must only contain Langchain::Message instance(s)" unless messages.is_a?(Array) && messages.all? { |m| m.is_a?(Messages::Base) }
117: 
118:       @messages = messages
119:     end
120: 
121:     # Add multiple messages
122:     #
123:     # @param messages [Array<Hash>] The messages to add
124:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
125:     def add_messages(messages:)
126:       messages.each do |message_hash|
127:         add_message(**message_hash.slice(:content, :role, :tool_calls, :tool_call_id))
128:       end
129:     end
130: 
131:     # Run the assistant
132:     #
133:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
134:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
135:     def run(auto_tool_execution: false)
136:       if messages.empty?
137:         Langchain.logger.warn("#{self.class} - No messages to process")
138:         @state = :completed
139:         return
140:       end
141: 
142:       @state = :in_progress
143:       @state = handle_state until run_finished?(auto_tool_execution)
144: 
145:       messages
146:     end
147: 
148:     # Run the assistant with automatic tool execution
149:     #
150:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
151:     def run!
152:       run(auto_tool_execution: true)
153:     end
154: 
155:     # Add a user message and run the assistant
156:     #
157:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
158:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
159:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
160:     def add_message_and_run(content: nil, image_url: nil, auto_tool_execution: false)
161:       add_message(content: content, image_url: image_url, role: "user")
162:       run(auto_tool_execution: auto_tool_execution)
163:     end
164: 
165:     # Add a user message and run the assistant with automatic tool execution
166:     #
167:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
168:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
169:     def add_message_and_run!(content: nil, image_url: nil)
170:       add_message_and_run(content: content, image_url: image_url, auto_tool_execution: true)
171:     end
172: 
173:     # Submit tool output
174:     #
175:     # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to submit output for
176:     # @param output [String] The output of the tool
177:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
178:     def submit_tool_output(tool_call_id:, output:)
179:       # TODO: Validate that `tool_call_id` is valid by scanning messages and checking if this tool call ID was invoked
180:       add_message(role: @llm_adapter.tool_role, content: output, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
181:     end
182: 
183:     # Delete all messages
184:     #
185:     # @return [Array] Empty messages array
186:     def clear_messages!
187:       # TODO: If this a bug? Should we keep the "system" message?
188:       @messages = []
189:     end
190: 
191:     # Set new instructions
192:     #
193:     # @param new_instructions [String] New instructions that will be set as a system message
194:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
195:     def instructions=(new_instructions)
196:       @instructions = new_instructions
197: 
198:       if @llm_adapter.support_system_message?
199:         # TODO: Should we still set a system message even if @instructions is "" or nil?
200:         replace_system_message!(content: new_instructions)
201:       end
202:     end
203: 
204:     # Set tool_choice, how tools should be selected
205:     #
206:     # @param new_tool_choice [String] Tool choice
207:     # @return [String] Selected tool choice
208:     def tool_choice=(new_tool_choice)
209:       validate_tool_choice!(new_tool_choice)
210:       @tool_choice = new_tool_choice
211:     end
212: 
213:     private
214: 
215:     # Replace old system message with new one
216:     #
217:     # @param content [String] New system message content
218:     # @return [Array<Langchain::Message>] The messages
219:     def replace_system_message!(content:)
220:       messages.delete_if(&:system?)
221:       return if content.nil?
222: 
223:       message = build_message(role: "system", content: content)
224:       messages.unshift(message)
225:     end
226: 
227:     # TODO: If tool_choice = "tool_function_name" and then tool is removed from the assistant, should we set tool_choice back to "auto"?
228:     def validate_tool_choice!(tool_choice)
229:       allowed_tool_choices = llm_adapter.allowed_tool_choices.concat(available_tool_names)
230:       unless allowed_tool_choices.include?(tool_choice)
231:         raise ArgumentError, "Tool choice must be one of: #{allowed_tool_choices.join(", ")}"
232:       end
233:     end
234: 
235:     # Check if the run is finished
236:     #
237:     # @param auto_tool_execution [Boolean] Whether or not to automatically run tools
238:     # @return [Boolean] Whether the run is finished
239:     def run_finished?(auto_tool_execution)
240:       finished_states = [:completed, :failed]
241: 
242:       requires_manual_action = (@state == :requires_action) && !auto_tool_execution
243:       finished_states.include?(@state) || requires_manual_action
244:     end
245: 
246:     # Handle the current state and transition to the next state
247:     #
248:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
249:     def handle_state
250:       case @state
251:       when :in_progress
252:         process_latest_message
253:       when :requires_action
254:         execute_tools
255:       end
256:     end
257: 
258:     # Process the latest message
259:     #
260:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
261:     def process_latest_message
262:       last_message = messages.last
263: 
264:       case last_message.standard_role
265:       when :system
266:         handle_system_message
267:       when :llm
268:         handle_llm_message
269:       when :user, :tool
270:         handle_user_or_tool_message
271:       else
272:         handle_unexpected_message
273:       end
274:     end
275: 
276:     # Handle system message scenario
277:     #
278:     # @return [Symbol] The completed state
279:     def handle_system_message
280:       Langchain.logger.warn("#{self.class} - At least one user message is required after a system message")
281:       :completed
282:     end
283: 
284:     # Handle LLM message scenario
285:     #
286:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
287:     def handle_llm_message
288:       messages.last.tool_calls.any? ? :requires_action : :completed
289:     end
290: 
291:     # Handle unexpected message scenario
292:     #
293:     # @return [Symbol] The failed state
294:     def handle_unexpected_message
295:       Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - Unexpected message role encountered: #{messages.last.standard_role}")
296:       :failed
297:     end
298: 
299:     # Handle user or tool message scenario by processing the LLM response
300:     #
301:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
302:     def handle_user_or_tool_message
303:       response = chat_with_llm
304: 
305:       add_message(role: response.role, content: response.chat_completion, tool_calls: response.tool_calls)
306:       record_used_tokens(response.prompt_tokens, response.completion_tokens, response.total_tokens)
307: 
308:       set_state_for(response: response)
309:     end
310: 
311:     def set_state_for(response:)
312:       if response.tool_calls.any?
313:         :in_progress
314:       elsif response.chat_completion
315:         :completed
316:       elsif response.completion # Currently only used by Ollama
317:         :completed
318:       else
319:         Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - LLM response does not contain tool calls, chat or completion response")
320:         :failed
321:       end
322:     end
323: 
324:     # Execute the tools based on the tool calls in the last message
325:     #
326:     # @return [Symbol] The next state
327:     def execute_tools
328:       run_tools(messages.last.tool_calls)
329:       :in_progress
330:     rescue => e
331:       Langchain.logger.error("#{self.class} - Error running tools: #{e.message}; #{e.backtrace.join('\n')}")
332:       :failed
333:     end
334: 
335:     # Call to the LLM#chat() method
336:     #
337:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::BaseResponse] The LLM response object
338:     def chat_with_llm
339:       Langchain.logger.debug("#{self.class} - Sending a call to #{llm.class}")
340: 
341:       params = @llm_adapter.build_chat_params(
342:         instructions: @instructions,
343:         messages: array_of_message_hashes,
344:         tools: @tools,
345:         tool_choice: tool_choice,
346:         parallel_tool_calls: parallel_tool_calls
347:       )
348:       @llm.chat(**params, &@block)
349:     end
350: 
351:     # Run the tools automatically
352:     #
353:     # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to run
354:     def run_tools(tool_calls)
355:       # Iterate over each function invocation and submit tool output
356:       tool_calls.each do |tool_call|
357:         run_tool(tool_call)
358:       end
359:     end
360: 
361:     # Run the tool call
362:     #
363:     # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call to run
364:     # @return [Object] The result of the tool call
365:     def run_tool(tool_call)
366:       tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments = @llm_adapter.extract_tool_call_args(tool_call: tool_call)
367: 
368:       tool_instance = tools.find do |t|
369:         t.class.tool_name == tool_name
370:       end or raise ArgumentError, "Tool: #{tool_name} not found in assistant.tools"
371: 
372:       # Call the callback if set
373:       tool_execution_callback.call(tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments) if tool_execution_callback # rubocop:disable Style/SafeNavigation
374: 
375:       output = tool_instance.public_send(method_name, **tool_arguments)
376: 
377:       # Handle both ToolResponse and legacy return values
378:       if output.is_a?(ToolResponse)
379:         add_message(role: @llm_adapter.tool_role, content: output.content, image_url: output.image_url, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
380:       else
381:         submit_tool_output(tool_call_id: tool_call_id, output: output)
382:       end
383:     end
384: 
385:     # Build a message
386:     #
387:     # @param role [String] The role of the message
388:     # @param content [String] The content of the message
389:     # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image to include in the message
390:     # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls to include in the message
391:     # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call to include in the message
392:     # @return [Langchain::Message] The Message object
393:     def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
394:       @llm_adapter.build_message(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
395:     end
396: 
397:     # Increment the tokens count based on the last interaction with the LLM
398:     #
399:     # @param prompt_tokens [Integer] The number of used prmopt tokens
400:     # @param completion_tokens [Integer] The number of used completion tokens
401:     # @param total_tokens [Integer] The total number of used tokens
402:     # @return [Integer] The current total tokens count
403:     def record_used_tokens(prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens_from_operation)
404:       @total_prompt_tokens += prompt_tokens if prompt_tokens
405:       @total_completion_tokens += completion_tokens if completion_tokens
406:       @total_tokens += total_tokens_from_operation if total_tokens_from_operation
407:     end
408: 
409:     def available_tool_names
410:       llm_adapter.available_tool_names(tools)
411:     end
412: 
413:     def validate_callback!(attr_name, callback)
414:       if !callback.nil? && !callback.respond_to?(:call)
415:         raise ArgumentError, "#{attr_name} must be a callable object, like Proc or lambda"
416:       end
417: 
418:       true
419:     end
420:   end
421: end

(End of file - total 421 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module LLM
6:       # TODO: Fix the message truncation when context window is exceeded
7:       class Adapter
8:         def self.build(llm)
9:           if llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::Anthropic)
10:             LLM::Adapters::Anthropic.new
11:           elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::AwsBedrock) && llm.defaults[:chat_model].include?("anthropic")
12:             LLM::Adapters::AwsBedrockAnthropic.new
13:           elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini) || llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::GoogleVertexAI)
14:             LLM::Adapters::GoogleGemini.new
15:           elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::MistralAI)
16:             LLM::Adapters::MistralAI.new
17:           elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::Ollama)
18:             LLM::Adapters::Ollama.new
19:           elsif llm.is_a?(Langchain::LLM::OpenAI)
20:             LLM::Adapters::OpenAI.new
21:           else
22:             raise ArgumentError, "Unsupported LLM type: #{llm.class}"
23:           end
24:         end
25:       end
26:     end
27:   end
28: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module LLM
6:       module Adapters
7:         class OpenAI < Base
8:           # Build the chat parameters for the OpenAI LLM
9:           #
10:           # @param messages [Array] The messages
11:           # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
12:           # @param tools [Array] The tools to use
13:           # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice
14:           # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether to make parallel tool calls
15:           # @return [Hash] The chat parameters
16:           def build_chat_params(
17:             messages:,
18:             instructions:,
19:             tools:,
20:             tool_choice:,
21:             parallel_tool_calls:
22:           )
23:             params = {messages: messages}
24:             if tools.any?
25:               params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
26:               params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
27:               # Temporary fix because OpenAI o1/o3/reasoning models don't support `parallel_tool_calls` parameter.
28:               # Set `Assistant.new(parallel_tool_calls: nil, ...)` to avoid the error.
29:               params[:parallel_tool_calls] = parallel_tool_calls unless parallel_tool_calls.nil?
30:             end
31:             params
32:           end
33: 
34:           # Build a OpenAI message
35:           #
36:           # @param role [String] The role of the message
37:           # @param content [String] The content of the message
38:           # @param image_url [String] The image URL
39:           # @param tool_calls [Array] The tool calls
40:           # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
41:           # @return [Messages::OpenAIMessage] The OpenAI message
42:           def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
43:             Messages::OpenAIMessage.new(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
44:           end
45: 
46:           # Extract the tool call information from the OpenAI tool call hash
47:           #
48:           # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash
49:           # @return [Array] The tool call information
50:           def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
51:             tool_call_id = tool_call.dig("id")
52: 
53:             function_name = tool_call.dig("function", "name")
54:             tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
55: 
56:             tool_arguments = tool_call.dig("function", "arguments")
57:             tool_arguments = if tool_arguments.is_a?(Hash)
58:               Langchain::Utils::HashTransformer.symbolize_keys(tool_arguments)
59:             else
60:               JSON.parse(tool_arguments, symbolize_names: true)
61:             end
62: 
63:             [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]
64:           end
65: 
66:           # Build the tools for the OpenAI LLM
67:           def build_tools(tools)
68:             tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_openai_format }.flatten
69:           end
70: 
71:           # Get the allowed assistant.tool_choice values for OpenAI
72:           def allowed_tool_choices
73:             ["auto", "none"]
74:           end
75: 
76:           # Get the available tool names for OpenAI
77:           def available_tool_names(tools)
78:             build_tools(tools).map { |tool| tool.dig(:function, :name) }
79:           end
80: 
81:           def tool_role
82:             Messages::OpenAIMessage::TOOL_ROLE
83:           end
84: 
85:           def support_system_message?
86:             Messages::OpenAIMessage::ROLES.include?("system")
87:           end
88: 
89:           private
90: 
91:           def build_tool_choice(choice)
92:             case choice
93:             when "auto"
94:               choice
95:             else
96:               {"type" => "function", "function" => {"name" => choice}}
97:             end
98:           end
99:         end
100:       end
101:     end
102:   end
103: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module LLM
6:       module Adapters
7:         class GoogleGemini < Base
8:           # Build the chat parameters for the Google Gemini LLM
9:           #
10:           # @param messages [Array] The messages
11:           # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
12:           # @param tools [Array] The tools to use
13:           # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice
14:           # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether to make parallel tool calls
15:           # @return [Hash] The chat parameters
16:           def build_chat_params(
17:             messages:,
18:             instructions:,
19:             tools:,
20:             tool_choice:,
21:             parallel_tool_calls:
22:           )
23:             Langchain.logger.warn "WARNING: `parallel_tool_calls:` is not supported by Google Gemini currently" if parallel_tool_calls
24: 
25:             params = {messages: messages}
26:             if tools.any?
27:               params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
28:               params[:system] = instructions if instructions
29:               params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_config(tool_choice)
30:             end
31:             params
32:           end
33: 
34:           # Build a Google Gemini message
35:           #
36:           # @param role [String] The role of the message
37:           # @param content [String] The content of the message
38:           # @param image_url [String] The image URL
39:           # @param tool_calls [Array] The tool calls
40:           # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
41:           # @return [Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage] The Google Gemini message
42:           def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
43:             Langchain.logger.warn "Image URL is not supported by Google Gemini" if image_url
44: 
45:             Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage.new(role: role, content: content, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
46:           end
47: 
48:           # Extract the tool call information from the Google Gemini tool call hash
49:           #
50:           # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash, format: {"functionCall"=>{"name"=>"weather__execute", "args"=>{"input"=>"NYC"}}}
51:           # @return [Array] The tool call information
52:           def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
53:             tool_call_id = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "name")
54:             function_name = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "name")
55:             tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
56:             tool_arguments = tool_call.dig("functionCall", "args").transform_keys(&:to_sym)
57:             [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]
58:           end
59: 
60:           # Build the tools for the Google Gemini LLM
61:           #
62:           # @param tools [Array<Langchain::Tool::Base>] The tools
63:           # @return [Array] The tools in Google Gemini format
64:           def build_tools(tools)
65:             tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_google_gemini_format }.flatten
66:           end
67: 
68:           # Get the allowed assistant.tool_choice values for Google Gemini
69:           def allowed_tool_choices
70:             ["auto", "none"]
71:           end
72: 
73:           # Get the available tool names for Google Gemini
74:           def available_tool_names(tools)
75:             build_tools(tools).map { |tool| tool.dig(:name) }
76:           end
77: 
78:           def tool_role
79:             Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage::TOOL_ROLE
80:           end
81: 
82:           def support_system_message?
83:             Messages::GoogleGeminiMessage::ROLES.include?("system")
84:           end
85: 
86:           private
87: 
88:           def build_tool_config(choice)
89:             case choice
90:             when "auto"
91:               {function_calling_config: {mode: "auto"}}
92:             when "none"
93:               {function_calling_config: {mode: "none"}}
94:             else
95:               {function_calling_config: {mode: "any", allowed_function_names: [choice]}}
96:             end
97:           end
98:         end
99:       end
100:     end
101:   end
102: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module LLM
6:       module Adapters
7:         class Anthropic < Base
8:           # Build the chat parameters for the Anthropic API
9:           #
10:           # @param messages [Array<Hash>] The messages
11:           # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
12:           # @param tools [Array<Hash>] The tools to use
13:           # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice
14:           # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether to make parallel tool calls
15:           # @return [Hash] The chat parameters
16:           def build_chat_params(
17:             messages:,
18:             instructions:,
19:             tools:,
20:             tool_choice:,
21:             parallel_tool_calls:
22:           )
23:             params = {messages: messages}
24:             if tools.any?
25:               params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
26:               params[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_choice, parallel_tool_calls)
27:             end
28:             params[:system] = instructions if instructions
29:             params
30:           end
31: 
32:           # Build an Anthropic message
33:           #
34:           # @param role [String] The role of the message
35:           # @param content [String] The content of the message
36:           # @param image_url [String] The image URL
37:           # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls
38:           # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
39:           # @return [Messages::AnthropicMessage] The Anthropic message
40:           def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
41:             Messages::AnthropicMessage.new(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
42:           end
43: 
44:           # Extract the tool call information from the Anthropic tool call hash
45:           #
46:           # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash, format: {"type"=>"tool_use", "id"=>"toolu_01TjusbFApEbwKPRWTRwzadR", "name"=>"news_retriever__get_top_headlines", "input"=>{"country"=>"us", "page_size"=>10}}], "stop_reason"=>"tool_use"}
47:           # @return [Array] The tool call information
48:           def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
49:             tool_call_id = tool_call.dig(:id)
50:             function_name = tool_call.dig(:name)
51:             tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
52:             tool_arguments = tool_call.dig(:input).transform_keys(&:to_sym)
53:             [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]
54:           end
55: 
56:           # Build the tools for the Anthropic API
57:           def build_tools(tools)
58:             tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_anthropic_format }.flatten
59:           end
60: 
61:           # Get the allowed assistant.tool_choice values for Anthropic
62:           def allowed_tool_choices
63:             ["auto", "any"]
64:           end
65: 
66:           # Get the available tool function names for Anthropic
67:           #
68:           # @param tools [Array<Hash>] The tools
69:           # @return [Array<String>] The tool function names
70:           def available_tool_names(tools)
71:             build_tools(tools).map { |tool| tool.dig(:name) }
72:           end
73: 
74:           def tool_role
75:             Messages::AnthropicMessage::TOOL_ROLE
76:           end
77: 
78:           def support_system_message?
79:             Messages::AnthropicMessage::ROLES.include?("system")
80:           end
81: 
82:           private
83: 
84:           def build_tool_choice(choice, parallel_tool_calls)
85:             tool_choice_object = {disable_parallel_tool_use: !parallel_tool_calls}
86: 
87:             case choice
88:             when "auto"
89:               tool_choice_object[:type] = "auto"
90:             when "any"
91:               tool_choice_object[:type] = "any"
92:             else
93:               tool_choice_object[:type] = "tool"
94:               tool_choice_object[:name] = choice
95:             end
96: 
97:             tool_choice_object
98:           end
99:         end
100:       end
101:     end
102:   end
103: end

2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module LLM
6:       module Adapters
7:         class Ollama < Base
8:           # Build the chat parameters for the Ollama LLM
9:           #
10:           # @param messages [Array] The messages
11:           # @param instructions [String] The system instructions
12:           # @param tools [Array] The tools to use
13:           # @param tool_choice [String] The tool choice
14:           # @param parallel_tool_calls [Boolean] Whether to make parallel tool calls
15:           # @return [Hash] The chat parameters
16:           def build_chat_params(
17:             messages:,
18:             instructions:,
19:             tools:,
20:             tool_choice:,
21:             parallel_tool_calls:
22:           )
23:             Langchain.logger.warn "WARNING: `parallel_tool_calls:` is not supported by Ollama currently" if parallel_tool_calls
24:             Langchain.logger.warn "WARNING: `tool_choice:` is not supported by Ollama currently" if tool_choice
25: 
26:             params = {messages: messages}
27:             if tools.any?
28:               params[:tools] = build_tools(tools)
29:             end
30:             params
31:           end
32: 
33:           # Build an Ollama message
34:           #
35:           # @param role [String] The role of the message
36:           # @param content [String] The content of the message
37:           # @param image_url [String] The image URL
38:           # @param tool_calls [Array] The tool calls
39:           # @param tool_call_id [String] The tool call ID
40:           # @return [Messages::OllamaMessage] The Ollama message
41:           def build_message(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
42:             Messages::OllamaMessage.new(role: role, content: content, image_url: image_url, tool_calls: tool_calls, tool_call_id: tool_call_id)
43:           end

45:           # Extract the tool call information from the OpenAI tool call hash
46:           #
47:           # @param tool_call [Hash] The tool call hash
48:           # @return [Array] The tool call information
49:           def extract_tool_call_args(tool_call:)
50:             tool_call_id = tool_call.dig("id")
51: 
52:             function_name = tool_call.dig("function", "name")
53:             tool_name, method_name = function_name.split("__")
54: 
55:             tool_arguments = tool_call.dig("function", "arguments")
56:             tool_arguments = if tool_arguments.is_a?(Hash)
57:               Langchain::Utils::HashTransformer.symbolize_keys(tool_arguments)
58:             else
59:               JSON.parse(tool_arguments, symbolize_names: true)
60:             end
61: 
62:             [tool_call_id, tool_name, method_name, tool_arguments]
63:           end
64: 
65:           # Build the tools for the Ollama LLM
66:           def available_tool_names(tools)
67:             build_tools(tools).map { |tool| tool.dig(:function, :name) }
68:           end
69: 
70:           # Get the allowed assistant.tool_choice values for Ollama
71:           def allowed_tool_choices
72:             ["auto", "none"]
73:           end
74: 
75:           def tool_role
76:             Messages::OllamaMessage::TOOL_ROLE
77:           end
78: 
79:           def support_system_message?
80:             Messages::OllamaMessage::ROLES.include?("system")
81:           end
82: 
83:           private
84: 
85:           def build_tools(tools)
86:             tools.map { |tool| tool.class.function_schemas.to_openai_format }.flatten
87:           end
88:         end
89:       end
90:     end
91:   end
92: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module Messages
6:       class OpenAIMessage < Base
7:         # OpenAI uses the following roles:
8:         ROLES = [
9:           "system",
10:           "assistant",
11:           "user",
12:           "tool"
13:         ].freeze
14: 
15:         TOOL_ROLE = "tool"
16: 
17:         # Initialize a new OpenAI message
18:         #
19:         # @param role [String] The role of the message
20:         # @param content [String] The content of the message
21:         # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image
22:         # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
23:         # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
24:         def initialize(
25:           role:,
26:           content: nil,
27:           image_url: nil,
28:           tool_calls: [],
29:           tool_call_id: nil
30:         )
31:           raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
32:           raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
33: 
34:           @role = role
35:           # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
36:           @content = content.to_s
37:           @image_url = image_url
38:           @tool_calls = tool_calls
39:           @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
40:         end
41: 
42:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
43:         #
44:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
45:         def llm?
46:           assistant?
47:         end
48: 
49:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
50:         #
51:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash
52:         def to_hash
53:           if assistant?
54:             assistant_hash
55:           elsif system?
56:             system_hash
57:           elsif tool?
58:             tool_hash
59:           elsif user?
60:             user_hash
61:           end
62:         end
63: 
64:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
65:         #
66:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
67:         def assistant?
68:           role == "assistant"
69:         end
70: 
71:         # Check if the message are system instructions
72:         #
73:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message are system instructions
74:         def system?
75:           role == "system"
76:         end
77: 
78:         # Check if the message is a tool call
79:         #
80:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
81:         def tool?
82:           role == "tool"
83:         end
84: 
85:         def user?
86:           role == "user"
87:         end
88: 
89:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
90:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "assistant"
91:         def assistant_hash
92:           if tool_calls.any?
93:             {
94:               role: "assistant",
95:               tool_calls: tool_calls
96:             }
97:           else
98:             {
99:               role: "assistant",
100:               content: build_content_array
101:             }
102:           end
103:         end
104: 
105:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
106:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "system"
107:         def system_hash
108:           {
109:             role: "system",
110:             content: build_content_array
111:           }
112:         end
113: 
114:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
115:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "tool"
116:         def tool_hash
117:           {
118:             role: "tool",
119:             tool_call_id: tool_call_id,
120:             content: build_content_array
121:           }
122:         end
123: 
124:         # Convert the message to an OpenAI API-compatible hash
125:         # @return [Hash] The message as an OpenAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
126:         def user_hash
127:           {
128:             role: "user",
129:             content: build_content_array
130:           }
131:         end
132: 
133:         # Builds the content value for the message hash
134:         # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes, with keys :type and :text or :image_url.
135:         def build_content_array
136:           content_details = []
137:           if content && !content.empty?
138:             content_details << {
139:               type: "text",
140:               text: content
141:             }
142:           end
143: 
144:           if image_url
145:             content_details << {
146:               type: "image_url",
147:               image_url: {
148:                 url: image_url
149:               }
150:             }
151:           end
152:           content_details
153:         end
154:       end
155:     end
156:   end
157: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module Messages
6:       class AnthropicMessage < Base
7:         ROLES = [
8:           "assistant",
9:           "user",
10:           "tool_result"
11:         ].freeze
12: 
13:         TOOL_ROLE = "tool_result"
14: 
15:         # Initialize a new Anthropic message
16:         #
17:         # @param role [String] The role of the message
18:         # @param content [String] The content of the message
19:         # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
20:         # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
21:         def initialize(
22:           role:,
23:           content: nil,
24:           image_url: nil,
25:           tool_calls: [],
26:           tool_call_id: nil
27:         )
28:           raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
29:           raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
30: 
31:           @role = role
32:           # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
33:           @content = content.to_s
34:           @image_url = image_url
35:           @tool_calls = tool_calls
36:           @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
37:         end
38: 
39:         # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
40:         #
41:         # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash
42:         def to_hash
43:           if assistant?
44:             assistant_hash
45:           elsif tool?
46:             tool_hash
47:           elsif user?
48:             user_hash
49:           end
50:         end
51: 
52:         # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
53:         #
54:         # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "assistant"
55:         def assistant_hash
56:           content_array = []
57:           if content && !content.empty?
58:             content_array << {type: "text", text: content}
59:           end
60: 
61:           {
62:             role: "assistant",
63:             content: content_array.concat(tool_calls)
64:           }
65:         end
66: 
67:         # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
68:         #
69:         # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
70:         def tool_hash
71:           {
72:             role: "user",
73:             # TODO: Tool can also return images
74:             # https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/tool-use#handling-tool-use-and-tool-result-content-blocks
75:             content: [
76:               {
77:                 type: "tool_result",
78:                 tool_use_id: tool_call_id,
79:                 content: content
80:               }
81:             ]
82:           }
83:         end
84: 
85:         # Convert the message to an Anthropic API-compatible hash
86:         #
87:         # @return [Hash] The message as an Anthropic API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
88:         def user_hash
89:           {
90:             role: "user",
91:             content: build_content_array
92:           }
93:         end
94: 
95:         # Builds the content value for the message hash
96:         # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes
97:         def build_content_array
98:           content_details = []
99: 
100:           if content && !content.empty?
101:             content_details << {
102:               type: "text",
103:               text: content
104:             }
105:           end
106: 
107:           if image
108:             content_details << {
109:               type: "image",
110:               source: {
111:                 type: "base64",
112:                 data: image.base64,
113:                 media_type: image.mime_type
114:               }
115:             }
116:           end
117: 
118:           content_details
119:         end
120: 
121:         # Check if the message is a tool call
122:         #
123:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
124:         def tool?
125:           role == "tool_result"
126:         end
127: 
128:         # Anthropic does not implement system prompts
129:         def system?
130:           false
131:         end
132: 
133:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
134:         #
135:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
136:         def assistant?
137:           role == "assistant"
138:         end
139: 
140:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
141:         #
142:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
143:         def llm?
144:           assistant?
145:         end
146:       end
147:     end
148:   end
149: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module Messages
6:       class GoogleGeminiMessage < Base
7:         # Google Gemini uses the following roles:
8:         ROLES = [
9:           "user",
10:           "model",
11:           "function"
12:         ].freeze
13: 
14:         TOOL_ROLE = "function"
15: 
16:         # Initialize a new Google Gemini message
17:         #
18:         # @param role [String] The role of the message
19:         # @param content [String] The content of the message
20:         # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
21:         # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
22:         def initialize(role:, content: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil)
23:           raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
24:           raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
25: 
26:           @role = role
27:           # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
28:           @content = content.to_s
29:           @tool_calls = tool_calls
30:           @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
31:         end
32: 
33:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
34:         #
35:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
36:         def llm?
37:           model?
38:         end
39: 
40:         # Convert the message to a Google Gemini API-compatible hash
41:         #
42:         # @return [Hash] The message as a Google Gemini API-compatible hash
43:         def to_hash
44:           if tool?
45:             tool_hash
46:           elsif model?
47:             model_hash
48:           elsif user?
49:             user_hash
50:           end
51:         end
52: 
53:         # Google Gemini does not implement system prompts
54:         def system?
55:           false
56:         end
57: 
58:         # Check if the message is a tool call
59:         #
60:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
61:         def tool?
62:           function?
63:         end
64: 
65:         # Check if the message is a user call
66:         #
67:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a user call
68:         def user?
69:           role == "user"
70:         end
71: 
72:         # Check if the message is a tool call
73:         #
74:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
75:         def function?
76:           role == "function"
77:         end
78: 
79:         # Convert the message to an GoogleGemini API-compatible hash
80:         # @return [Hash] The message as an GoogleGemini API-compatible hash, with the role as "model"
81:         def model_hash
82:           {
83:             role: role,
84:             parts: build_parts
85:           }
86:         end
87: 
88:         # Convert the message to an GoogleGemini API-compatible hash
89:         # @return [Hash] The message as an GoogleGemini API-compatible hash, with the role as "function"
90:         def tool_hash
91:           {
92:             role: role,
93:             parts: [{
94:               functionResponse: {
95:                 name: tool_call_id,
96:                 response: {
97:                   name: tool_call_id,
98:                   content: content
99:                 }
100:               }
101:             }]
102:           }
103:         end
104: 
105:         # Convert the message to an GoogleGemini API-compatible hash
106:         # @return [Hash] The message as an GoogleGemini API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
107:         def user_hash
108:           {
109:             role: role,
110:             parts: build_parts
111:           }
112:         end
113: 
114:         # Builds the part value for the message hash
115:         # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes of the text or of the tool calls if present
116:         def build_parts
117:           if tool_calls.any?
118:             tool_calls
119:           else
120:             [{text: content}]
121:           end
122:         end
123: 
124:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
125:         #
126:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
127:         def model?
128:           role == "model"
129:         end
130:       end
131:     end
132:   end
133: end

22:         # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message

25:           raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
26:           raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
27: 
28:           # Ensure compatibility with `URI::RFC2396_PARSER` (Ruby 3.4+) and `URI::DEFAULT_PARSER` (<= 3.3).
29:           uri_parser = defined?(URI::RFC2396_PARSER) ? URI::RFC2396_PARSER : URI::DEFAULT_PARSER

31:

45:             h[:role] = role

47:             h[:images] = [image.base64] if image

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain
4:   class Assistant
5:     module Messages
6:       class MistralAIMessage < Base
7:         # MistralAI uses the following roles:
8:         ROLES = [
9:           "system",
10:           "assistant",
11:           "user",
12:           "tool"
13:         ].freeze
14: 
15:         TOOL_ROLE = "tool"
16: 
17:         # Initialize a new MistralAI message
18:         #
19:         # @param role [String] The role of the message
20:         # @param content [String] The content of the message
21:         # @param image_url [String] The URL of the image
22:         # @param tool_calls [Array<Hash>] The tool calls made in the message
23:         # @param tool_call_id [String] The ID of the tool call
24:         def initialize(role:, content: nil, image_url: nil, tool_calls: [], tool_call_id: nil) # TODO: Implement image_file: reference (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/messages/object#messages/object-content)
25:           raise ArgumentError, "Role must be one of #{ROLES.join(", ")}" unless ROLES.include?(role)
26:           raise ArgumentError, "Tool calls must be an array of hashes" unless tool_calls.is_a?(Array) && tool_calls.all? { |tool_call| tool_call.is_a?(Hash) }
27: 
28:           @role = role
29:           # Some Tools return content as a JSON hence `.to_s`
30:           @content = content.to_s
31:           # Make sure you're using the Pixtral model if you want to send image_url
32:           @image_url = image_url
33:           @tool_calls = tool_calls
34:           @tool_call_id = tool_call_id
35:         end
36: 
37:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
38:         #
39:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
40:         def llm?
41:           assistant?
42:         end
43: 
44:         # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
45:         #
46:         # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash
47:         def to_hash
48:           if assistant?
49:             assistant_hash
50:           elsif system?
51:             system_hash
52:           elsif tool?
53:             tool_hash
54:           elsif user?
55:             user_hash
56:           end
57:         end
58: 
59:         # Check if the message came from an LLM
60:         #
61:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message was produced by an LLM
62:         def assistant?
63:           role == "assistant"
64:         end
65: 
66:         # Check if the message are system instructions
67:         #
68:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message are system instructions
69:         def system?
70:           role == "system"
71:         end
72: 
73:         # Check if the message is a tool call
74:         #
75:         # @return [Boolean] true/false whether this message is a tool call
76:         def tool?
77:           role == "tool"
78:         end
79: 
80:         # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
81:         # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "assistant"
82:         def assistant_hash
83:           {
84:             role: "assistant",
85:             content: content,
86:             tool_calls: tool_calls,
87:             prefix: false
88:           }
89:         end
90: 
91:         # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
92:         # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "system"
93:         def system_hash
94:           {
95:             role: "system",
96:             content: build_content_array
97:           }
98:         end
99: 
100:         # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
101:         # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "tool"
102:         def tool_hash
103:           {
104:             role: "tool",
105:             content: content,
106:             tool_call_id: tool_call_id
107:           }
108:         end
109: 
110:         # Convert the message to an MistralAI API-compatible hash
111:         # @return [Hash] The message as an MistralAI API-compatible hash, with the role as "user"
112:         def user_hash
113:           {
114:             role: "user",
115:             content: build_content_array
116:           }
117:         end
118: 
119:         # Builds the content value for the message hash
120:         # @return [Array<Hash>] An array of content hashes, with keys :type and :text or :image_url.
121:         def build_content_array
122:           content_details = []
123: 
124:           if content && !content.empty?
125:             content_details << {
126:               type: "text",
127:               text: content
128:             }
129:           end
130: 
131:           if image_url
132:             content_details << {
133:               type: "image_url",
134:               image_url: image_url
135:             }
136:           end
137: 
138:           content_details
139:         end
140:       end
141:     end
142:   end
143: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require "openai"
4: 
5: module Langchain::LLM
6:   # LLM interface for OpenAI APIs: https://platform.openai.com/overview
7:   #
8:   # Gem requirements:
9:   #    gem "ruby-openai", "~> 6.3.0"
10:   #
11:   # Usage:
12:   #    llm = Langchain::LLM::OpenAI.new(
13:   #      api_key: ENV["OPENAI_API_KEY"],
14:   #      llm_options: {}, # Available options: https://github.com/alexrudall/ruby-openai/blob/main/lib/openai/client.rb#L5-L13
15:   #      default_options: {}
16:   #    )
17:   class OpenAI < Base
18:     DEFAULTS = {
19:       n: 1,
20:       chat_model: "gpt-4o-mini",
21:       embedding_model: "text-embedding-3-small"
22:     }.freeze
23: 
24:     EMBEDDING_SIZES = {
25:       "text-embedding-ada-002" => 1536,
26:       "text-embedding-3-large" => 3072,
27:       "text-embedding-3-small" => 1536
28:     }.freeze
29: 
30:     # Initialize an OpenAI LLM instance
31:     #
32:     # @param api_key [String] The API key to use
33:     # @param client_options [Hash] Options to pass to the OpenAI::Client constructor
34:     def initialize(api_key:, llm_options: {}, default_options: {})
35:       depends_on "ruby-openai", req: "openai"
36: 
37:       llm_options[:log_errors] = Langchain.logger.debug? unless llm_options.key?(:log_errors)
38: 
39:       @client = ::OpenAI::Client.new(access_token: api_key, **llm_options) do |f|
40:         f.response :logger, Langchain.logger, {headers: true, bodies: true, errors: true}
41:       end
42: 
43:       @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
44:       chat_parameters.update(
45:         model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
46:         logprobs: {},
47:         top_logprobs: {},
48:         n: {default: @defaults[:n]},
49:         temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
50:         user: {},
51:         response_format: {default: @defaults[:response_format]}
52:       )
53:       chat_parameters.ignore(:top_k)
54:     end
55: 
56:     # Generate an embedding for a given text
57:     #
58:     # @param text [String] The text to generate an embedding for
59:     # @param model [String] ID of the model to use
60:     # @param encoding_format [String] The format to return the embeddings in. Can be either float or base64.
61:     # @param user [String] A unique identifier representing your end-user
62:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse] Response object
63:     def embed(
64:       text:,
65:       model: defaults[:embedding_model],
66:       encoding_format: nil,
67:       user: nil,
68:       dimensions: @defaults[:dimensions]
69:     )
70:       raise ArgumentError.new("text argument is required") if text.empty?
71:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?
72:       raise ArgumentError.new("encoding_format must be either float or base64") if encoding_format && %w[float base64].include?(encoding_format)
73: 
74:       parameters = {
75:         input: text,
76:         model: model
77:       }
78:       parameters[:encoding_format] = encoding_format if encoding_format
79:       parameters[:user] = user if user
80: 
81:       if dimensions
82:         parameters[:dimensions] = dimensions
83:       elsif EMBEDDING_SIZES.key?(model)
84:         parameters[:dimensions] = EMBEDDING_SIZES[model]
85:       end
86: 
87:       # dimensions parameter not supported by text-embedding-ada-002 model
88:       parameters.delete(:dimensions) if model == "text-embedding-ada-002"
89: 
90:       response = with_api_error_handling do
91:         client.embeddings(parameters: parameters)
92:       end
93: 
94:       Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse.new(response)
95:     end
96: 
97:     # rubocop:disable Style/ArgumentsForwarding
98:     # Generate a completion for a given prompt
99:     #
100:     # @param prompt [String] The prompt to generate a completion for
101:     # @param params [Hash] The parameters to pass to the `chat()` method
102:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse] Response object
103:     # @deprecated Use {chat} instead.
104:     def complete(prompt:, **params)
105:       Langchain.logger.warn "DEPRECATED: `Langchain::LLM::OpenAI#complete` is deprecated, and will be removed in the next major version. Use `Langchain::LLM::OpenAI#chat` instead."
106: 
107:       if params[:stop_sequences]
108:         params[:stop] = params.delete(:stop_sequences)
109:       end
110:       # Should we still accept the `messages: []` parameter here?
111:       messages = [{role: "user", content: prompt}]
112:       chat(messages: messages, **params)
113:     end
114: 
115:     # rubocop:enable Style/ArgumentsForwarding
116: 
117:     # Generate a chat completion for given messages.
118:     #
119:     # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
120:     # @option params [Array<Hash>] :messages List of messages comprising the conversation so far
121:     # @option params [String] :model ID of the model to use
122:     def chat(params = {}, &block)
123:       parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
124: 
125:       raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(parameters[:messages]).empty?
126:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if parameters[:model].to_s.empty?
127:       if parameters[:tool_choice] && Array(parameters[:tools]).empty?
128:         raise ArgumentError.new("'tool_choice' is only allowed when 'tools' are specified.")
129:       end
130: 
131:       if block
132:         @response_chunks = []
133:         parameters[:stream_options] = {include_usage: true}
134:         parameters[:stream] = proc do |chunk, _bytesize|
135:           chunk_content = chunk.dig("choices", 0) || {}
136:           @response_chunks << chunk
137:           yield chunk_content
138:         end
139:       end
140: 
141:       response = with_api_error_handling do
142:         client.chat(parameters: parameters)
143:       end
144: 
145:       response = response_from_chunks if block
146:       reset_response_chunks
147: 
148:       Langchain::LLM::Response::OpenAIResponse.new(response)
149:     end
150: 
151:     # Generate a summary for a given text
152:     #
153:     # @param text [String] The text to generate a summary for
154:     # @return [String] The summary
155:     def summarize(text:)
156:       prompt_template = Langchain::Prompt.load_from_path(
157:         file_path: Langchain.root.join("langchain/llm/prompts/summarize_template.yaml")
158:       )
159:       prompt = prompt_template.format(text: text)
160: 
161:       complete(prompt: prompt)
162:     end
163: 
164:     def default_dimensions
165:       @defaults[:dimensions] || EMBEDDING_SIZES.fetch(defaults[:embedding_model])
166:     end
167: 
168:     private
169: 
170:     attr_reader :response_chunks
171: 
172:     def reset_response_chunks
173:       @response_chunks = []
174:     end
175: 
176:     def with_api_error_handling
177:       response = yield
178:       return if response.nil? || response.empty?
179: 
180:       raise Langchain::LLM::ApiError.new "OpenAI API error: #{response.dig("error", "message")}" if response&.dig("error")
181: 
182:       response
183:     end
184: 
185:     def response_from_chunks
186:       grouped_chunks = @response_chunks
187:         .group_by { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "index") }
188:         .except(nil) # the last chunk (that contains the token usage) has no index
189:       final_choices = grouped_chunks.map do |index, chunks|
190:         {
191:           "index" => index,
192:           "message" => {
193:             "role" => "assistant",
194:             "content" => chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "content") }.join,
195:             "tool_calls" => tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
196:           }.compact,
197:           "finish_reason" => chunks.last.dig("choices", 0, "finish_reason")
198:         }
199:       end
200:       @response_chunks.first&.slice("id", "object", "created", "model")&.merge({"choices" => final_choices, "usage" => @response_chunks.last["usage"]})
201:     end
202: 
203:     def tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(choice_chunks)
204:       tool_call_chunks = choice_chunks.select { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls") }
205:       return nil if tool_call_chunks.empty?
206: 
207:       tool_call_chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "index") }.map do |index, chunks|
208:         first_chunk = chunks.first
209: 
210:         {
211:           "id" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "id"),
212:           "type" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "type"),
213:           "function" => {
214:             "name" => first_chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "function", "name"),
215:             "arguments" => chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("choices", 0, "delta", "tool_calls", 0, "function", "arguments") }.join
216:           }
217:         }
218:       end
219:     end
220:   end
221: end

(End of file - total 221 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain::LLM
4:   class ApiError < StandardError; end
5: 
6:   # A LLM is a language model consisting of a neural network with many parameters (typically billions of weights or more), trained on large quantities of unlabeled text using self-supervised learning or semi-supervised learning.
7:   #
8:   # Langchain.rb provides a common interface to interact with all supported LLMs:
9:   #
10:   # - {Langchain::LLM::Anthropic}
11:   # - {Langchain::LLM::Azure}
12:   # - {Langchain::LLM::Cohere}
13:   # - {Langchain::LLM::GoogleGemini}
14:   # - {Langchain::LLM::GoogleVertexAI}
15:   # - {Langchain::LLM::HuggingFace}
16:   # - {Langchain::LLM::OpenAI}
17:   # - {Langchain::LLM::Replicate}
18:   #
19:   # @abstract
20:   class Base
21:     include Langchain::DependencyHelper
22: 
23:     # A client for communicating with the LLM
24:     attr_accessor :client
25: 
26:     # Default LLM options. Can be overridden by passing `default_options: {}` to the Langchain::LLM::* constructors.
27:     attr_reader :defaults
28: 
29:     # Ensuring backward compatibility for {default_dimensions}.
30:     #
31:     # @deprecated Use {default_dimensions} instead.
32:     # @see https://github.com/patterns-ai-core/langchainrb/pull/586
33:     def default_dimension
34:       Langchain.logger.warn "DEPRECATED: `default_dimension` is deprecated, and will be removed in the next major version. Please use `default_dimensions` instead."
35: 
36:       default_dimensions
37:     end
38: 
39:     # Returns the number of vector dimensions used by DEFAULTS[:chat_model]
40:     #
41:     # @return [Integer] Vector dimensions
42:     def default_dimensions
43:       self.class.const_get(:DEFAULTS).dig(:dimensions)
44:     end
45: 
46:     #
47:     # Generate a chat completion for a given prompt. Parameters will depend on the LLM
48:     #
49:     # @raise NotImplementedError if not supported by the LLM
50:     def chat(...)
51:       raise NotImplementedError, "#{self.class.name} does not support chat"
52:     end
53: 
54:     #
55:     # Generate a completion for a given prompt. Parameters will depend on the LLM.
56:     #
57:     # @raise NotImplementedError if not supported by the LLM
58:     def complete(...)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Langchain::LLM
4:   #
5:   # Wrapper around Anthropic APIs.
6:   #
7:   # Gem requirements:
8:   #   gem "anthropic", "~> 1.10.0"
9:   #
10:   # Usage:
11:   #     llm = Langchain::LLM::Anthropic.new(api_key: ENV["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"])
12:   #
13:   class Anthropic < Base
14:     DEFAULTS = {
15:       temperature: 0.0,
16:       completion_model: "claude-2.1",
17:       chat_model: "claude-sonnet-4-6",
18:       max_tokens: 256
19:     }.freeze
20: 
21:     # Initialize an Anthropic LLM instance
22:     #
23:     # @param api_key [String] The API key to use
24:     # @param llm_options [Hash] Options to pass to the Anthropic client
25:     # @param default_options [Hash] Default options to use on every call to LLM, e.g.: { temperature:, completion_model:, chat_model:, max_tokens:, thinking: }
26:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Anthropic] Langchain::LLM::Anthropic instance
27:     def initialize(api_key:, llm_options: {}, default_options: {})
28:       depends_on "anthropic"
29: 
30:       @client = ::Anthropic::Client.new(api_key: api_key, **llm_options)
31:       @defaults = DEFAULTS.merge(default_options)
32:       chat_parameters.update(
33:         model: {default: @defaults[:chat_model]},
34:         temperature: {default: @defaults[:temperature]},
35:         max_tokens: {default: @defaults[:max_tokens]},
36:         metadata: {},
37:         system: {},
38:         thinking: {default: @defaults[:thinking]},
39:         request_options: {}
40:       )
41:       chat_parameters.ignore(:n, :user)
42:       chat_parameters.remap(stop: :stop_sequences)
43:     end
44: 
45:     # Generate a completion for a given prompt
46:     #
47:     # @param prompt [String] Prompt to generate a completion for
48:     # @param model [String] The model to use
49:     # @param max_tokens_to_sample [Integer] The maximum number of tokens to sample
50:     # @param stop_sequences [Array<String>] The stop sequences to use
51:     # @param temperature [Float] The temperature to use
52:     # @param top_p [Float] The top p value to use
53:     # @param top_k [Integer] The top k value to use
54:     # @param metadata [Hash] The metadata to use
55:     # @param stream [Boolean] Whether to stream the response
56:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The completion
57:     def complete(
58:       prompt:,
59:       model: @defaults[:completion_model],
60:       max_tokens: @defaults[:max_tokens],
61:       stop_sequences: nil,
62:       temperature: @defaults[:temperature],
63:       top_p: nil,
64:       top_k: nil,
65:       metadata: nil,
66:       stream: nil
67:     )
68:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if model.empty?
69:       raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if max_tokens.nil?
70: 
71:       parameters = {
72:         model: model,
73:         prompt: prompt,
74:         max_tokens_to_sample: max_tokens,
75:         temperature: temperature
76:       }
77:       parameters[:stop_sequences] = stop_sequences if stop_sequences
78:       parameters[:top_p] = top_p if top_p
79:       parameters[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
80:       parameters[:metadata] = metadata if metadata
81:       parameters[:stream] = stream if stream
82: 
83:       response = with_api_error_handling do
84:         client.complete(parameters: parameters)
85:       end
86: 
87:       Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
88:     end
89: 
90:     # Generate a chat completion for given messages
91:     #
92:     # @param [Hash] params unified chat parmeters from [Langchain::LLM::Parameters::Chat::SCHEMA]
93:     # @option params [Array<String>] :messages Input messages
94:     # @option params [String] :model The model that will complete your prompt
95:     # @option params [Integer] :max_tokens Maximum number of tokens to generate before stopping
96:     # @option params [Hash] :metadata Object describing metadata about the request
97:     # @option params [Array<String>] :stop_sequences Custom text sequences that will cause the model to stop generating
98:     # @option params [Boolean] :stream Whether to incrementally stream the response using server-sent events
99:     # @option params [String] :system System prompt
100:     # @option params [Float] :temperature Amount of randomness injected into the response
101:     # @option params [Array<String>] :tools Definitions of tools that the model may use
102:     # @option params [Hash] :thinking Enable extended thinking mode, e.g. { type: "enabled", budget_tokens: 4000 }
103:     # @option params [Integer] :top_k Only sample from the top K options for each subsequent token
104:     # @option params [Float] :top_p Use nucleus sampling.
105:     # @return [Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse] The chat completion
106:     def chat(params = {}, &block)
107:       parameters = chat_parameters.to_params(params)
108: 
109:       raise ArgumentError.new("messages argument is required") if Array(parameters[:messages]).empty?
110:       raise ArgumentError.new("model argument is required") if parameters[:model].empty?
111:       raise ArgumentError.new("max_tokens argument is required") if parameters[:max_tokens].nil?
112: 
113:       if block
114:         @response_chunks = []
115:         parameters[:stream] = proc do |chunk|
116:           @response_chunks << chunk
117:           yield chunk
118:         end
119:       end
120: 
121:       response = client.messages.create(parameters)
122: 
123:       response = response_from_chunks if block
124:       reset_response_chunks
125: 
126:       Langchain::LLM::Response::AnthropicResponse.new(response)
127:     end
128: 
129:     def with_api_error_handling
130:       response = yield
131:       return if response.empty?
132: 
133:       raise Langchain::LLM::ApiError.new "Anthropic API error: #{response.dig("error", "message")}" if response&.dig("error")
134: 
135:       response
136:     end
137: 
138:     def response_from_chunks
139:       grouped_chunks = @response_chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.except(nil)
140: 
141:       usage_chunk = @response_chunks.find { |chunk| chunk["type"] == "message_delta" }
142:       usage = usage_chunk&.dig("usage")&.transform_keys(&:to_sym)
143:       stop_reason = usage_chunk&.dig("delta", "stop_reason")
144: 
145:       content = grouped_chunks.map do |_index, chunks|
146:         text = chunks.map { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "text") }.join
147:         if !text.nil? && !text.empty?
148:           {type: "text", text: text}
149:         else
150:           tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
151:         end
152:       end.flatten
153: 
154:       first_chunk = @response_chunks.first
155:       {
156:         id: first_chunk&.dig("id") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "id"),
157:         model: first_chunk&.dig("model") || first_chunk&.dig("message", "model"),
158:         content: content,
159:         usage: usage,
160:         role: "assistant",
161:         stop_reason: stop_reason
162:       }
163:     end
164: 
165:     def tool_calls_from_choice_chunks(chunks)
166:       return unless (first_block = chunks.find { |chunk| chunk.dig("content_block", "type") == "tool_use" })
167: 
168:       chunks.group_by { |chunk| chunk["index"] }.map do |index, chunks|
169:         input = chunks.select { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }
170:           .map! { |chunk| chunk.dig("delta", "partial_json") }.join
171:         {
172:           id: first_block.dig("content_block", "id"),
173:           type: "tool_use",
174:           name: first_block.dig("content_block", "name"),
175:           input: input.empty? ? nil : JSON.parse(input).transform_keys(&:to_sym)
176:         }
177:       end.compact
178:     end
179: 
180:     private
181: 
182:     def reset_response_chunks
183:       @response_chunks = []
184:     end
185:   end
186: end

(End of file - total 186 lines)
</content>
